plan: Reduce SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% to enhance income and stability, favoring a conservative approach.
Trim SMH and QQQ to 5% and 10% respectively due to their downtrend status.
Maintain a significant cash buffer to protect capital and provide flexibility.
Focus on broad index and income for a low-drawdown portfolio.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower volatility. Expect smaller drawdowns but also more modest growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and reduce overall portfolio risk.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure, improving overall portfolio stability.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ to 10% each due to their downtrend status, reallocating capital.
Initiate new positions in ZS and ASML, leveraging their high scanner scores for growth.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer to manage risk and seize new opportunities.
Balance broad index exposure with quality tech names for risk-first growth.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing high-quality growth names. Expect moderate drawdowns and a blend of stability and growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to adhere to concentration limits, freeing up capital for high-conviction plays.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop, reallocating capital to stronger setups.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ to 5% each due to their downtrend, focusing on uptrending assets.
Initiate significant new positions in ZS, ASML, and NVDA, concentrating in top scanner setups.
Lean into high-scoring growth and chip names, utilizing leverage where appropriate.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer, accepting higher risk for potential stretched growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth. It concentrates in high-scoring setups and uses leverage, which amplifies both upside and drawdown. Be aware of increased volatility, potential for significant losses, and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged products.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and free capital for income-focused assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing low drawdown.
Significantly increase QQQI to 30% to maximize monthly distributions and yield.
Reduce QQQ and CRWD to 5% each, shifting away from growth and high-beta names.
Sell SMH to exit a downtrending sector, further de-risking the portfolio.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer to ensure stability and liquidity for income needs.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, emphasizing steady distributions and low drawdown. It significantly reduces growth and leverage in favor of income-generating ETFs. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs; this is not a savings account.
